483	\section{Affine acyclicity of the {\v C}ech complex}
484	
485	\begin{definition}
486	[Standard affine cover from a spanning family]
487	  \label{def:standard_affine_cover}
488	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop}
489	  \mathlibok
490	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open}).}
491	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring (so \(R = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R,
492	  \mathcal{O})\)) and let \(s : \iota \to R\) be a family of elements that generate
493	  the unit ideal, \(\operatorname{Ideal.span}(\operatorname{range} s) = \top\). The
494	  \emph{standard affine open cover} of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) associated with
495	  \(s\) is the affine open cover whose index set is \(\iota\), whose \(i\)-th member
496	  is the basic open \(D(s_i)\), and whose \(i\)-th structure morphism is the map of
497	  affine schemes induced by the localisation homomorphism \(R \to R_{s_i}\); that
498	  is, in Mathlib notation the defining equation
499	  \(\bigl(\text{affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop}\bigr)_f\, i =
500	  \operatorname{Spec.map}\bigl(\text{algebraMap}\; R\; (\operatorname{Localization.Away}(s_i))\bigr)\),
501	  so that the sections over the \(i\)-th piece are the \emph{away} localisation
502	  \(R_{s_i} = \operatorname{Localization.Away}(s_i)\) and the geometric piece is
503	  \(D(s_i)\). This is exactly the standard open covering
504	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R = \bigcup_i D(s_i)\) by basic opens, packaged as a single
505	  cover object. It is the spanning-family bundle \((s, hs)\) that the affine
506	  {\v C}ech vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} is stated over.
507	\end{definition}
508	
509	\subsection{The categorical--module bridge (L1)}
510	\label{sec:cech_l1_bridge}
511	
512	The affine vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} is a statement about the
513	\emph{section} {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
514	\mathcal{F})\) of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} --- a cochain complex of
515	abelian groups --- whereas the combinatorial contracting-homotopy core operates on a
516	concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules. The passage between the two, the
517	\emph{categorical--module bridge}, decomposes into two self-contained statements: the
518	section identification (each section group is an away localisation, with the
519	restriction maps the canonical localisation maps) and the homology-to-exactness
520	reduction (vanishing of the abstract homology is equivalent to exactness of the
521	underlying localised-module complex). These are isolated as
522	Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} and
523	Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} below; the assembly --- feeding each
524	positive-degree node through the local-to-global exactness criterion and closing with
525	the dependent combinatorial port --- is carried out in the proof of
526	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}.
527	
528	\begin{definition}
529	
530	[Quasi-coherent sections over a basic open are an away localisation]
531	  \label{def:qcoh_sections_localized}
532	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.qcohSectionsAwayLocalized,
533	    AlgebraicGeometry.basicOpen_sprod,
534	    AlgebraicGeometry.iInf_fin_succ,
535	    AlgebraicGeometry.qcohRestriction_eq_comparison}
536	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:standard_affine_cover}
537	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Lemma \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (Tag 01HV),
538	  % items (4)--(5) (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L701--707).
539	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "(4) For every $f\in R$ we have $\Gamma(D(f), \widetilde M) = M_f$
540	  % as an $R_f$-module.
541	  % (5) Whenever $D(g) \subset D(f)$ the restriction mappings
542	  % on $\mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}$ and $\widetilde M$
543	  % are the maps
544	  % $R_f \to R_g$ and $M_f \to M_g$ from Lemma
545	  % \ref{lemma-standard-open}."
546	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (Tag 01HV),
547	  items (4)--(5).}
548	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
549	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R}\)-module, and write
550	  \(M = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})\) for its module of global
551	  sections. For every \(g \in R\), the restriction-of-sections map
552	  \(M = \mathcal{F}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \to \mathcal{F}(D(g))\) exhibits the
553	  section group \(\mathcal{F}(D(g))\) as the away localisation \(M_g\) of \(M\); that
554	  is, this map is an \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) for the multiplicative
555	  submonoid \(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} g\) of powers of \(g\). Moreover, for
556	  \(D(g_2) \subseteq D(g_1)\) the restriction map
557	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(g_1)) \to \mathcal{F}(D(g_2))\) is, under these identifications, the
558	  canonical localisation map \(M_{g_1} \to M_{g_2}\) (the unique \(R\)-linear map
559	  compatible with the localisation units). Item~(4) of Tag~01HV gives the first
560	  assertion for \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) (\(\Gamma(D(g), \widetilde{M}) = M_g\)
561	  as an \(R_g\)-module) and item~(5) gives the second (the restriction along
562	  \(D(g) \subset D(f)\) is the localisation map \(M_f \to M_g\)).
563	
564	  The module-level input on the side of Mathlib is the development
565	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde}: the comparison map
566	  \((\operatorname{tilde.toOpen} M\, (D(g))).\mathrm{hom}\) carries the instance
567	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} g)\), and
568	  \(\operatorname{isUnit\_algebraMap\_end\_basicOpen}\) records that \(g\) acts
569	  invertibly on the sections over \(D(g)\); the restriction compatibility is
570	  \(\operatorname{tilde.toOpen\_res}\). These supply the statement \emph{verbatim} for
571	  the standard sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\). For an \emph{arbitrary} quasi-coherent
572	  \(\mathcal{F}\) the remaining step is the canonical comparison
573	  \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})}\), i.e.\
574	  that the counit \(\operatorname{Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism. This
575	  is \emph{not} yet available in Mathlib for a general quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\):
576	  Mathlib provides it only from a \emph{global} presentation
577	  (\(\operatorname{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_of\_presentation}\)), whereas quasi-coherence
578	  supplies only \emph{local} presentation data on a cover. Globalising the local data
579	  on the affine \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) --- the affine equivalence
580	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \simeq \operatorname{Mod}(R)\), Stacks
581	  Tag~01I8 --- is the one genuine gap this definition records as a build target; it is
582	  the canonical missing affine equivalence and is self-contained.
583	
584	  Two combinatorial--geometric ingredients package the identification into the form
585	  consumed by Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}. First, the multi-index
586	  intersection identity
587	  \(\bigl(\bigcap_k D(s_{\sigma(k)})\bigr) = D\bigl(\prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\bigr)\) in
588	  \((\operatorname{Spec} R).\mathrm{Opens}\) (\(\operatorname{basicOpen\_sprod}\), proved
589	  by induction on the index length using the infimum-splitting helper
590	  \(\operatorname{iInf\_fin\_succ}\) and \(\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen\_mul}\)):
591	  it rewrites the \((p+1)\)-fold intersection over a multi-index \(\sigma\) as the single
592	  basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\), so the
593	  section group there is the away localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\). Second, the restriction
594	  compatibility of item~(5) is made explicit: for a basic-open inclusion
595	  \(D(b) \subseteq D(a)\) the presheaf restriction map of \(\widetilde{M}\) between the
596	  two section groups \emph{is} the away-localisation comparison
597	  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison}\) of the two localisation units
598	  (\(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\), by the uniqueness
599	  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison\_unique}\) of a map of localised modules);
600	  this is the single differential brick that the {\v C}ech coface match of
601	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} feeds on.
602	\end{definition}
603	
604	\begin{lemma}
605	[Section {\v C}ech homology vanishes iff the localised complex is exact]
606	  \label{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}
607	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_homology_exact,
608	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts,
609	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.isUnit_powers,
610	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.mul,
611	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd,
612	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison,
613	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_apply,
614	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp,
615	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply,
616	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp_structure,
617	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_self,
618	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_unique,
619	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechCoeff,
620	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechCoeff_transport_eq_comparison,
621	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechCoface,
622	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact,
623	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechPrepend,
624	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cech_hcomm,
625	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cech_hsh,
626	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cech_hu,
627	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.sprod,
628	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.sprod_cons,
629	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.sprod_succAbove_dvd}
630	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized, lem:section_cech_module_exact,
631	    lem:section_cech_product_equiv, lem:section_cech_objd_apply,
632	    lem:section_cech_coface_match, lem:section_cech_ab_exact}
633	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
634	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (the {\v C}ech complex is
635	  % the complex of localisations) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
636	  % L58--67).
637	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
638	  % $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
639	  % is identified with the complex
640	  % $$
641	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
642	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
643	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
644	  % \ldots
645	  % $$"
646	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
647	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (identification with the
648	  complex of localisations).}
649	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec}
650	  R}\)-module and \(\mathcal{U}\) the standard cover associated with a spanning family
651	  \(s : \iota \to R\). Under the section identification of
652	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}, the section {\v C}ech complex
653	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
654	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} is isomorphic, as a cochain complex, to
655	  the concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules
656	  \[
657	    D^\bullet : \quad
658	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
659	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0,1\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
660	    \cdots,
661	    \qquad
662	    s_\sigma = \prod_{k} s_{\sigma(k)},
663	  \]
664	  whose differential is the alternating sum of the canonical localisation maps. The
665	  intended top-level conclusion is the directed homology vanishing
666	  \[
667	    1 \leq p
668	    \;\Longrightarrow\;
669	    \operatorname{IsZero}\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
670	      \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p\bigr),
671	  \]
672	  which is the form the affine vanishing
673	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} (\(\operatorname{sectionCech\_affine\_vanishing}\))
674	  consumes; it is read off from positive-degree exactness of the underlying group
675	  homomorphisms,
676	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\bigl(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to D^{p+1}\bigr)\), which is
677	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}.
678	
679	  The reduction from the abstract homology to this group-level exactness is \emph{not}
680	  a \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}\) statement: the degree-\(p\) object of
681	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) is the \emph{categorical
682	  product}
683	  \(\prod^{c}_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \iota}
684	  \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) in the category \(\mathrm{Ab}\) of abelian groups (the
685	  alternating-coface-map complex of the \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-valued cosimplicial object
686	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\)), not an \(R\)-module object, so the
687	  module-category criterion \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.moduleCat\_exact\_iff}\) does
688	  not apply. The correct chain runs through three steps, isolated as the sub-lemmas
689	  below: \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the homology is \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) at
690	  \(p\) (\(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\)); \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\)
691	  of a short complex in \(\mathrm{Ab}\) is the underlying-group exactness
692	  \(\forall x_2,\; g(x_2) = 0 \Rightarrow \exists x_1,\; f(x_1) = x_2\) via the abelian
693	  analogue \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\)
694	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}); and the categorical product is moved to the
695	  per-\(\sigma\) localised modules by the element-level product equivalence
696	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\prod^{c} F_\sigma) \simeq \prod_\sigma
697	  \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma)\)
698	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}), under which the {\v C}ech coface
699	  differential matches the localisation differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of
700	  \(D^\bullet\) (the abstract cosimplicial unfold
701	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply} followed by the tilde-bridge identification
702	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}). Transporting the
703	  already-proved exactness \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\) of
704	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact} across the product equivalence then
705	  discharges the group-level exactness. The active target is the tilde case
706	  \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\), for which the per-coordinate comparisons are
707	  immediate from \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\); a general
708	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) is reduced to this case via the affine equivalence
709	  \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma\mathcal{F}}\) (Stacks Tag~01I8), the
710	  separately-deferred globalisation gap recorded in
711	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}.
712	\end{lemma}
713	
714	\begin{proof}
715	  \uses{def:qcoh_sections_localized, def:section_cech_complex}
716	  The term identification is the degreewise statement of
717	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: the degree-\(p\) section group
718	  \(\prod_\sigma \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is \(\prod_\sigma M_{s_\sigma}\), since
719	  each \((p+1)\)-fold intersection \(D(s_{\sigma(0)}) \cap \cdots \cap
720	  D(s_{\sigma(p)})\) is the basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product
721	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\) and the sections there are the away
722	  localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\). The differential identification is item~(5) of
723	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: each face restriction
724	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j})) \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is the
725	  canonical localisation map \(M_{s_{\sigma \circ d_j}} \to M_{s_\sigma}\), so the
726	  alternating sum of restrictions is the alternating sum of localisation maps, the
727	  differential of \(D^\bullet\) (the coface match of
728	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}). The homology vanishing then follows in
729	  three steps. First, \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\) turns
730	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the homology at \(p\) into \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) of
731	  the short complex at \(p\). Second, because the degree-\(p\) objects are
732	  \emph{categorical products in \(\mathrm{Ab}\)} rather than \(R\)-modules, the abelian
733	  analogue \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\)
734	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}) --- not the module-category criterion ---
735	  reduces \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) to the underlying-group exactness
736	  \(\forall x_2,\; g(x_2) = 0 \Rightarrow \exists x_1,\; f(x_1) = x_2\). Third, the
737	  element-level product equivalence
738	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\prod^{c} F_\sigma) \simeq \prod_\sigma
739	  \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma)\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
740	  identifies each product group with the per-\(\sigma\) localised module \(D^p\), so the
741	  group-level exactness is exactly \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to
742	  D^{p+1})\), supplied by \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\)
743	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}).
744	
745	  The identification of the abstract section complex with the concrete localised
746	  complex \(D^\bullet\) --- together with the construction of the prepend transport
747	  \(c\) and the localisation cofaces \(\delta_k\) discharging the unit, shift, and
748	  coface-commutation compatibilities \(\mathtt{hu}\)/\(\mathtt{hsh}\)/\(\mathtt{hcomm}\)
749	  of the dependent combinatorial port --- is the bulk of the bridge. The non-circular
750	  route is reflection along the localisation equivalence on its essential image (the
751	  global-sections functor \(\Gamma\) is the inverse equivalence there, so it reflects
752	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of homology); it is \emph{not} the assertion that
753	  ``\(\Gamma\) preserves homology'', which would presuppose the very affine exactness
754	  being proved.
755	\end{proof}

757	\begin{lemma}
758	
759	\leanok
760	[Element-level product equivalence for the section {\v C}ech terms]
761	  \label{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
762	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechProductEquiv,
763	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechProductEquiv_apply}

765	  Let \(\{F_\sigma\}_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \iota}\) be the finite family of
766	  abelian groups \(F_\sigma = \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) appearing in degree \(p\) of
767	  the section {\v C}ech complex of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex}. There is
768	  an equivalence of underlying types
769	  \[
770	    \operatorname{ToType}\Bigl(\prod^{c}_{\sigma} F_\sigma\Bigr)
771	    \;\simeq\;
772	    \prod_{\sigma} \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma),
773	  \]
774	  between the elements of the categorical product \(\prod^{c}_{\sigma} F_\sigma\) in
775	  \(\mathrm{Ab}\) and the dependent functions \(\sigma \mapsto\) (element of
776	  \(F_\sigma\)). It is supplied by the Mathlib concrete-limit comparison
777	  \(\operatorname{CategoryTheory.Limits.Concrete.productEquiv}\), which holds because the
778	  forgetful functor \(\operatorname{forget}\,\mathrm{Ab}\) preserves the discrete
779	  product (\(\operatorname{PreservesLimit}(\operatorname{Discrete.functor} F)\)). Under
780	  this equivalence each coordinate
781	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma)))\) is identified with the away
782	  localisation \(M_{s_\sigma} = \operatorname{dCoeff}\) of
783	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex} by the
784	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) uniqueness of
785	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} (both
786	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) and \(M_{s_\sigma}\) localise \(M\) at \(s_\sigma\), so
787	  the comparison is the canonical isomorphism). This is the move between the
788	  categorical product of \(\mathrm{Ab}\) and the per-\(\sigma\) localised modules.
789	  The coordinate projection of the equivalence is recorded separately as the
790	  definitional restatement
791	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv\_apply}\): the \(\sigma\)-component of the
792	  image of \(y\) under the equivalence is the underlying group map of the categorical
793	  projection \(\pi_\sigma\) applied to \(y\).
794	\end{lemma}
795	
796	\begin{proof}
797	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized}
798	  \leanok
799	  The type-level equivalence is \(\operatorname{Concrete.productEquiv}\) applied to the
800	  family \(\sigma \mapsto F_\sigma\); its hypothesis is that
801	  \(\operatorname{forget}\,\mathrm{Ab}\) preserves the discrete-diagram limit, which is
802	  the standard instance for \(\mathrm{Ab}\). The per-coordinate identification
803	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))) \cong M_{s_\sigma}\) is the
804	  uniqueness clause of \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\): by
805	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} the restriction map
806	  \(M \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) and the localisation unit
807	  \(M \to M_{s_\sigma}\) are both localisations of \(M\) at
808	  \(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers}(s_\sigma)\)
809	  (\(\operatorname{qcohSectionsAwayLocalized}\)), hence canonically isomorphic over
810	  \(M\). Assembling the coordinatewise isomorphisms after the product equivalence gives
811	  the stated identification.
812	\end{proof}

814	\begin{lemma}
815	[Abstract cosimplicial unfold of the section {\v C}ech differential]
816	  \label{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}
817	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_objD_apply,
818	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechFaceRestr}
819	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
820	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and
821	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an open cover. For a multi-index
822	  \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(q+2) \to I\) and a face index
823	  \(i \in \mathrm{Fin}(q+2)\), write
824	  \[
825	    \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i) :
826	      \mathcal{F}\Bigl(\,\bigcap_{l} U_{(\sigma \circ d_i)(l)}\Bigr)
827	      \;\longrightarrow\;
828	      \mathcal{F}\Bigl(\,\bigcap_{k} U_{\sigma(k)}\Bigr)
829	  \]
830	  for the \(i\)-th presheaf face restriction --- the value of \(\mathcal{F}\) on the
831	  inclusion of opens
832	  \(\bigcap_{k} U_{\sigma(k)} \subseteq \bigcap_{l} U_{(\sigma \circ d_i)(l)}\)
833	  obtained from the order-preserving coface \(d_i\) of the simplex category. Then,
834	  read through the product equivalence
835	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}\) of
836	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}, the underlying group action of the
837	  degree-\(q\) differential
838	  \(\operatorname{objD} q\) of the alternating-coface-map complex
839	  \(\operatorname{AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex}
840	  (\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\,\mathcal{U}\,\mathcal{F})\) is the alternating
841	  sum of the face restrictions applied to the deleted-face coordinates:
842	  \[
843	    \bigl(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}_{q+1}\,(\operatorname{objD} q\, t)\bigr)_\sigma
844	      \;=\;
845	      \sum_{i = 0}^{q+1} (-1)^i \,
846	        \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)
847	          \Bigl(\bigl(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}_{q}\, t\bigr)_{\sigma \circ d_i}\Bigr).
848	  \]
849	  This is \emph{purely cosimplicial}: it records the shape of the section {\v C}ech
850	  differential as an alternating sum of presheaf restrictions, with \emph{no}
851	  sheaf-theoretic or localisation identification of those restrictions. It is the
852	  abstract shape that the localisation differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of
853	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex} matches once the tilde-bridge
854	  identification of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match} is supplied.
855	\end{lemma}
856	
857	\begin{proof}
858	  \uses{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
859	  Unfold the degree-\(q\) map of \(\operatorname{alternatingCofaceMapComplex}\) on
860	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\): by construction \(\operatorname{objD} q\)
861	  is the alternating sum \(\sum_{i} (-1)^i\, d^i\) of the cosimplicial cofaces, each
862	  coface \(d^i\) being the \(\operatorname{Pi.lift}\) whose \(\sigma\)-component
863	  projects to the coordinate \(\sigma \circ d_i\) and post-composes with the presheaf
864	  restriction \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\). Reading the
865	  \(\sigma\)-coordinate through the product equivalence (the coordinate-projection
866	  restatement \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv\_apply}\)) commutes the projection
867	  past the categorical sum: applying the sum of \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-morphisms to an element
868	  distributes over the sum (the finite-sum-application identity
869	  \(\operatorname{ab\_hom\_finsetSum\_apply}\)), and each summand becomes
870	  \((-1)^i\) times the face restriction of the \((\sigma \circ d_i)\)-coordinate. The
871	  per-coordinate projection of the cosimplicial coface is the \(\operatorname{Pi.lift}\)
872	  computation \(\operatorname{Pi.lift\_π}\), which identifies the projected coface with
873	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) precomposed with the projection to
874	  \(\sigma \circ d_i\). Assembling the signs yields the stated alternating sum.
875	\end{proof}

877	\begin{lemma}
878	[Coface match: the section {\v C}ech differential is the localisation differential]
879	  \label{lem:section_cech_coface_match}
880	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechCofaceMatch}
881	  \uses{lem:section_cech_objd_apply, def:qcoh_sections_localized, def:section_cech_module_complex}
882	  Take \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\), the standard affine cover \(\mathcal{U}\)
883	  associated with a spanning family \(s : \iota \to R\), and \(\mathcal{F} =
884	  \widetilde{M}\) the quasi-coherent sheaf associated with an \(R\)-module \(M\)
885	  (for a general quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\), see the note following the statement). In
886	  the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}, the degree-\(p\)
887	  differential of the section {\v C}ech complex
888	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\), read through the product
889	  equivalence, is exactly the localisation differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of the
890	  module complex \(D^\bullet\) of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex}.
891	  Concretely, under the per-coordinate identifications each section group
892	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is the away localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\) and each face
893	  restriction \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) is the away-localisation
894	  coface \(\operatorname{dCoface}\), so the alternating sum of face restrictions of
895	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply} becomes the alternating sum of localisation
896	  cofaces, i.e.\ \(\operatorname{dDiff}\).
897	\end{lemma}
898	
899	% NOTE: For the tilde case \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) the per-coordinate
900	% comparison isomorphism and the naturality square follow directly from
901	% \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\). For a general quasi-coherent
902	% \(\mathcal{F}\), reduce to the tilde case via \(\mathcal{F} \cong
903	% \widetilde{\Gamma\mathcal{F}}\) (Stacks Tag 01I8), the affine equivalence
904	% \(\operatorname{QCoh}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \simeq \operatorname{Mod}(R)\),
905	% which is the open gap recorded in Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}.
906	\begin{proof}
907	  \uses{lem:section_cech_objd_apply, def:section_cech_module_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized}
908	  The proof layers in two steps.
909	
910	  \emph{(i) Abstract unfold.} By Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}, the
911	  \(\sigma\)-coordinate of the degree-\(p\) differential, read through the product
912	  equivalence, is the alternating sum
913	  \(\sum_{i} (-1)^i\, \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) of presheaf face
914	  restrictions applied to the deleted-face coordinates. This fixes the shape of the
915	  differential without any sheaf identification of the restrictions.
916	
917	  \emph{(ii) Tilde \(\mathcal{F}\)-bridge.} For \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) each
918	  face restriction is identified with the localisation coface
919	  \(\operatorname{SectionCechModule.dCoface}\) by transporting along a per-coordinate
920	  isomorphism. The section group
921	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) and the localised module
922	  \(M_{s_\sigma} = \operatorname{LocalizedModule}(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers}
923	  s_\sigma)\, M\) both localise \(M\) at the submonoid of powers of
924	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\): the former by
925	  \(\operatorname{qcohSectionsAwayLocalized}\) together with the multi-index
926	  intersection identity \(\operatorname{basicOpen\_sprod}\) (which rewrites
927	  \(\bigcap_k D(s_{\sigma(k)})\) as the single basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\)), the latter
928	  by construction. Hence there is a canonical \(R\)-linear isomorphism
929	  \[
930	    \varphi_\sigma :
931	      \operatorname{ToType}\bigl(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\bigr)
932	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
933	      M_{s_\sigma},
934	  \]
935	  the comparison of the two \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) structures, unique by
936	  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison\_unique}\) and supplied by
937	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule.iso}\). The naturality square
938	  \[
939	    \varphi_\sigma \;\circ\; \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)
940	      \;=\;
941	      \operatorname{dCoface} \;\circ\; \varphi_{\sigma \circ d_i}
942	  \]
943	  is exactly \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\) of
944	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: the presheaf restriction of
945	  \(\widetilde{M}\) between the two section groups is the away-localisation comparison
946	  of the two localisation units. Substituting (ii) into (i) and summing with signs
947	  identifies the section {\v C}ech differential with \(\operatorname{dDiff}\).
948	
949	  The isomorphism \(\varphi_\sigma\) is obtained by matching the two descriptions of
950	  the sections of \(\widetilde{M}\) over \(D(s_\sigma)\): the cosimplicial functor
951	  provides one description and the localisation property of
952	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} provides the other. Their agreement,
953	  together with the naturality square, is the content of this bridge.
954	\end{proof}

1	\chapter{Higher direct images $R^i f_*$ of quasi-coherent sheaves ($i \geq 1$)}
2	\label{chap:Cohomology_HigherDirectImage}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/HigherDirectImage.lean
4	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, definition of $R^i f_*$ as the
5	% $i$-th right derived functor of $f_*$ (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex
6	% and references/stacks-coherent.md).
7	
8	\section{Setup and motivation}
9	
10	This chapter records the derived-functor higher direct images \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\)
11	for \(i \geq 1\). It provides the single object that the {\v C}ech computation of
12	Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage} compares against: the comparison
13	theorem \(\ref{lem:cech_computes_cohomology}\) identifies the cohomology of the
14	relative {\v C}ech complex with \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) wherever the derived functor
15	is defined.
16	
17	We use the classical cohomology-sheaf formulation (not the derived category).
18	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a
19	quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module.
20	
21	\section{Definition of the higher direct images}
22	
23	\begin{definition}\leanok
24	[Higher direct image]
25	  \label{def:higher_direct_image}
26	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage}
27	  % NOTE (iter-233): the Lean `higherDirectImage` carries an explicit instance
28	  % hypothesis `[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]`, because Mathlib has
29	  % `Abelian X.Modules` but NOT `EnoughInjectives` / `IsGrothendieckAbelian` for
30	  % `SheafOfModules` over a sheaf of rings (the Grothendieck-abelian instance is
31	  % only for `Sheaf J A` with a fixed value category). The definition is therefore
32	  % conditional; the hypothesis can be dropped once a lane proves that instance.
33	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
34	  % `lemma-relative-affine-vanishing' (proof), describing $R^i f_*$ as the
35	  % $i$-th right derived functor of $f_*$ via the sheafified presheaf
36	  % $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$
37	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L188--192).
38	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "According to
39	  % Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
40	  % the sheaf
41	  % $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
42	  % $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$."
43	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, \S\,Quasi-coherence of
44	  higher direct images (and the underlying Cohomology chapter).}
45	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a
46	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. For \(i \geq 0\) the \emph{\(i\)-th
47	  higher direct image} \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) is the \(i\)-th right derived functor
48	  of the pushforward functor \(f_*\) applied to \(\mathcal{F}\). Concretely,
49	  \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) is the \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module obtained as the
50	  sheafification of the presheaf
51	  \[
52	    V \;\longmapsto\; H^i\bigl(f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
53	    \qquad V \subset S \text{ open},
54	  \]
55	  where \(H^i(-, -)\) denotes sheaf cohomology. For \(i = 0\) this recovers the
56	  ordinary pushforward \(R^0 f_*\mathcal{F} = f_*\mathcal{F}\).
57	\end{definition}
58

956	\begin{lemma}
957	[Abelian-group exactness criterion for the section {\v C}ech complex]
958	  \label{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}
959	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechAbExact,
960	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact,
961	    AlgebraicGeometry.ab_hom_finsetSum_apply}
962	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, lem:section_cech_objd_apply,
963	    lem:section_cech_product_equiv, lem:section_cech_coface_match,
964	    lem:section_cech_module_exact}
965	  For \(1 \leq p\) the homology of the section {\v C}ech complex
966	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
967	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} vanishes,
968	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
969	  \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p)\). The argument splits into a homological
970	  precursor and an unconditional ladder transport.
971	  \begin{itemize}
972	    \item The precursor \(\operatorname{sectionCech\_isZero\_homology\_of\_objD\_exact}\)
973	      is pure homological algebra: \emph{given}
974	      \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) of the underlying group homomorphisms of two
975	      consecutive coface differentials \(\operatorname{objD} q\),
976	      \(\operatorname{objD}(q+1)\), the homology of the section complex at degree
977	      \(q+1\) is zero. It chains
978	      \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\),
979	      \(\operatorname{HomologicalComplex.exactAt\_iff'}\) (to expose the short complex
980	      around the node), and the abelian-group exactness criterion
981	      \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff\_function\_exact}\) (which states that
982	      a short complex of abelian groups is exact iff for every \(x_2\) with
983	      \(g(x_2) = 0\) there is \(x_1\) with \(f(x_1) = x_2\)).
984	    \item The unconditional \(\operatorname{sectionCechAbExact}\) discharges that
985	      hypothesis by transporting the already-proved \(R\)-module exactness
986	      \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\) of
987	      Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact} across the degreewise additive
988	      isomorphism, using the ladder-transport lemma
989	      \(\operatorname{Function.Exact.of\_ladder\_addEquiv\_of\_exact}\).
990	  \end{itemize}
991	\end{lemma}
992	
993	\begin{proof}
994	  \uses{lem:section_cech_objd_apply, lem:section_cech_product_equiv,
995	    lem:section_cech_coface_match, lem:section_cech_module_exact}
996	  Fix \(p = q + 1 \geq 1\). By the precursor
997	  \(\operatorname{sectionCech\_isZero\_homology\_of\_objD\_exact}\) it suffices to
998	  exhibit \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) of the underlying group homomorphisms of the
999	  two consecutive coface differentials \(\operatorname{objD} q\),
1000	  \(\operatorname{objD}(q+1)\) of the section complex.
1001	
1002	  This exactness is obtained by the ladder transport
1003	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact.of\_ladder\_addEquiv\_of\_exact}\). The vertical maps
1004	  of the ladder are the degreewise additive isomorphisms
1005	  \(e_p = \prod_\sigma \varphi_\sigma \circ \operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}_p\):
1006	  the product equivalence of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv} followed by the
1007	  coordinatewise comparison \(\varphi_\sigma\) identifying each section group with the
1008	  localised module \(M_{s_\sigma}\). The two commuting squares of the ladder are the
1009	  coface match: the abstract unfold of the differential as an alternating sum of face
1010	  restrictions (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}, the decl
1011	  \(\operatorname{sectionCech\_objD\_apply}\)) together with the per-coordinate
1012	  identification of each face restriction with the localisation coface
1013	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}) shows
1014	  \(e_{p+1} \circ \operatorname{objD} p = \operatorname{dDiff}^{\,p} \circ e_p\). The
1015	  horizontal exactness fed into the transport is the \(R\)-module exactness
1016	  \(H = \operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}.
1017	  Transporting \(H\) across the additive ladder yields the desired
1018	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) of the \(\operatorname{objD}\) maps, discharging the
1019	  hypothesis of the precursor.
1020	\end{proof}

1023	[The un-localised section {\v C}ech module complex \(D^\bullet\)]
1024	  \label{def:section_cech_module_complex}

1050	  the global sections of a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec}
1051	  R}\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\), and \(s : \iota \to R\) (\(\iota\) finite) a spanning
1052	  family of the unit ideal, \(\operatorname{Ideal.span}(\operatorname{range} s) =
1053	  \top\) (Definition~\ref{def:standard_affine_cover}). The \emph{un-localised section
1054	  {\v C}ech module complex} \(D^\bullet\) is the cochain complex of \(R\)-modules
1055	  whose degree-\(p\) term is the product over multi-indices
1056	  \(\sigma : \{0, \ldots, p\} \to \iota\) of the away localisations
1057	  \[
1058	    D^p \;=\; \prod_{\sigma} M_{s_\sigma},
1059	    \qquad s_\sigma = \prod_{k} s_{\sigma(k)},
1060	    \qquad M_{s_\sigma} = \operatorname{LocalizedModule}
1061	      \bigl(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers}(s_\sigma)\bigr)\, M,

1063	  with differential \(d^p : D^p \to D^{p+1}\) the alternating sum
1064	  \(\sum_{j} (-1)^j\, \delta^j\) of the coface comparison maps \(\delta^j\), each of
1065	  which is the canonical away-localisation comparison
1066	  \(M_{s_{\sigma \circ d_j}} \to M_{s_\sigma}\) sending a fraction to its image under
1067	  the further localisation at the omitted factor. The single-coefficient comparison
1068	  map and its localisation property are recorded by the \(\mathtt{dCoface}\) and
1069	  \(\mathtt{dCoeff}\) data and the differential by \(\mathtt{dDiff}\) (with action
1070	  \(\mathtt{dDiff\_apply}\)); the auxiliary \(\mathtt{spanIdx}\)/\(\mathtt{spanIdx\_spec}\)
1071	  select a witnessing index of the spanning relation.

1074	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} and to its localisations by three
1075	  comparison families. First, the maps \(\mathtt{dToCech}\) identify each term
1076	  \(D^p\) with the degree-\(p\) section group

1079	  (\(\mathtt{dToCech\_isLocalizedModule}\)), intertwining the differentials
1080	  (\(\mathtt{cechCoface\_dToCech}\), \(\mathtt{dToCech\_comm}\)); the section-side
1081	  cofaces are \(\mathtt{cechCofaceLin}\) with action \(\mathtt{cechCoface\_apply}\).
1082	  Second, localising \(D^\bullet\) at a spanning element \(s_r\) reproduces the
1083	  \emph{localised} {\v C}ech complex: the localised differential \(\mathtt{locDiff}\)
1084	  (action \(\mathtt{locDiff\_apply}\)) agrees, via
1085	  \(\mathtt{locDiff\_eq\_depDiff}\), with the dependent-coefficient combinatorial
1086	  differential, and its exactness is recorded as \(\mathtt{locDiff\_exact}\). The
1087	  localisation comparison maps \(\mathtt{fLoc}\) (action \(\mathtt{fLoc\_apply}\))
1088	  exhibit each localised term as a localised module
1089	  (\(\mathtt{fLoc\_isLocalizedModule}\)) intertwining \(\mathtt{dDiff}\) with
1090	  \(\mathtt{locDiff}\) (\(\mathtt{locDiff\_fLoc}\), \(\mathtt{map\_dDiff\_eq\_locDiff}\)).
1091	  The key algebraic fact underlying all three families is the localisation
1092	  transitivity \(M_a[1/b] = M_{ab}\): localising the away module \(M_{s_\sigma}\)
1093	  further at \(s_r\) gives \(M_{s_r s_\sigma}\). This is the keystone
1094	  \(\mathtt{AwayComparison.comparison\_isLocalizedModule}\) (the composite of two away
1095	  localisations is again the away localisation at the product), with the cancellation
1096	  bookkeeping \(\mathtt{AwayComparison.Inverts.smul\_pow\_cancel}\). Thus localising
1097	  \(D^\bullet\) at \(s_r\) (an \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule.Away}\)) reproduces

1102	\begin{lemma}
1103	
1104	\leanok
1105	[Positive-degree exactness of the section {\v C}ech module complex]
1106	  \label{lem:section_cech_module_exact}
1107	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact}
1108	  \uses{def:section_cech_module_complex}
1109	  With the notation of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex}, the
1110	  un-localised section {\v C}ech module complex \(D^\bullet\) is exact in positive
1111	  degrees: for every \(p\),
1112	  \[
1113	    \operatorname{Function.Exact}\bigl(d^{p+1} : D^{p+1} \to D^{p+2},\;
1114	      d^{p+2} : D^{p+2} \to D^{p+3}\bigr).
1115	  \]
1116	  This is step~(a) of the affine vanishing: the node-by-node exactness of the
1117	  un-localised module complex, from which the homology vanishing of the section
1118	  {\v C}ech complex (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}, step~(c)) is read off.
1119	\end{lemma}

1121	\begin{proof}
1122	  \uses{def:section_cech_module_complex}
1123	  \leanok
1124	  Exactness of \(D^\bullet\) at each positive-degree node is a local-to-global
1125	  statement over the spanning family. By the local-to-global exactness criterion
1126	  \(\operatorname{exact\_of\_isLocalized\_span}(\operatorname{Set.range} s)\) (with the

1131	  differential of \(D^\bullet\) with the localised Čech differential \(\mathtt{locDiff}\)

1134	  \(\mathtt{AwayComparison.comparison\_isLocalizedModule}\)). Exactness of that

1314	\subsection{Presheaf-level {\v C}ech machinery}
1315	\label{sec:presheaf_cech_machinery}
1316	
1317	The {\v C}ech-acyclicity of injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
1318	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} below) is not a statement about a single
1319	affine cover: it is proved on the abelian category \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)
1320	of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, where the {\v C}ech complex of sections
1321	is the \(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex of an explicit free resolution. The argument
1322	rests on two presheaf-level facts, recorded here, and one transfer of injectivity.
1323	First, a free complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of presheaves of modules whose
1324	\(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex into \(\mathcal{F}\) \emph{is} the {\v C}ech complex
1325	of sections (Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex},
1326	Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} and
1327	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_hom_identification}). Second, the resolution property of
1328	that complex: \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) resolves \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\)
1329	concentrated in degree \(0\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso}). Together
1330	these give: an injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is injective as a presheaf
1331	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} via
1332	Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_of_adjoint} applied to the sheafification adjunction
1333	Lemma~\ref{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}), so \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, \mathcal{I})\) is
1334	exact and carries the augmented resolution to an exact section complex, i.e.\
1335	\(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\). This direct route uses
1336	neither enough-injectives in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) nor a right-derived
1337	\(\delta\)-functor identification.

1354	  % L1095--1135).

1376	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology,
1377	  \texttt{lemma-cech-map-into} (the free complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\)).}
1378	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an
1379	  open covering, with \(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p} : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\)
1380	  the open immersion of each finite intersection. The Stacks formulation uses, for
1381	  each intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\), the \emph{extension-by-zero} presheaf
1382	  \((j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_{!}\,\mathcal{O}_X|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\), the presheaf of
1383	  modules sending an open \(W\) to \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\) when
1384	  \(W \subset U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) and to \(0\) otherwise. This extension-by-zero
1385	  presheaf is, canonically, the \emph{free presheaf of modules on the representable}
1386	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\): writing
1387	  \(\mathbf{y}\,V = \operatorname{Hom}(-, V)\) for the Yoneda presheaf of the open
1388	  \(V\) on the site of opens of \(X\), the free presheaf of modules
1389	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V)\) sends \(W\) to the free
1390	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\)-module on the set \(\operatorname{Hom}(W, V)\), which is
1391	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\) if \(W \subset V\) and \(0\) otherwise — exactly
1392	  extension-by-zero of \(\mathcal{O}_X|_V\). Its defining universal property
1393	  \[
1394	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)}\bigl(
1395	      \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V),\, \mathcal{F}\bigr)
1396	    \;\cong\; \mathcal{F}(V)
1397	  \]
1398	  is the free–forgetful adjunction for presheaves of modules composed with the
1399	  Yoneda isomorphism \(\operatorname{Hom}(\mathbf{y}\,V, G) \cong G(V)\). The
1400	  \emph{free presheaf complex} of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the chain complex
1401	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) in the abelian category
1402	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules whose
1403	  term in degree \(p\) is the direct sum
1404	  \[
1405	    K(\mathcal{U})_p
1406	      = \bigoplus_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
1407	        \operatorname{free}\bigl(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
1408	  \]
1409	  with \(K(\mathcal{U})_p\) placed in degree \(p \geq 0\) (and \(0\) in negative
1410	  degrees), and whose differential \(K(\mathcal{U})_{p+1} \to K(\mathcal{U})_p\) is
1411	  the alternating sum \(\sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\) of the maps
1412	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}) \to
1413	  \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}})\)
1414	  obtained by applying \(\operatorname{free}\) to the representable index-dropping
1415	  maps \(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} \to \mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots
1416	  \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}\) induced by the inclusions of opens
1417	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} \subset U_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}\)
1418	  that drop the index \(i_j\). Each summand is a \emph{projective} object of
1419	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\), because the universal property above identifies
1420	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots
1421	  i_p}), -)\) with the exact ``sections over \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\)'' functor
1422	  \((-)(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\); this is the structural feature exploited below.
1423	
1424	  We assume the index set \(I\) is \emph{finite} (so the required coproducts exist in
1425	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\); this matches the finiteness hypothesis of the
1426	  comparison target). Here the direct sum \(\bigoplus\) is the categorical coproduct
1427	  \(\coprod\) (the indexed \(\operatorname{Sigma}\) colimit), which for a finite index
1428	  set coincides with the finite biproduct.
1429	\end{definition}

1465	  covering. For a presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{F}\), the
1466	  \emph{section {\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},

1478	    d(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}
1479	      = \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\,
1480	        s_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}
1481	        \big|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}}.
1482	  \]
1483	  This object is \emph{distinct} from the relative {\v C}ech complex
1484	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
1485	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex}: the latter is the pushforward complex with terms
1486	  \(\prod (f|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\) in
1487	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\), an object of relative sheaves over the base, whereas the
1488	  section {\v C}ech complex here is the simpler complex of \emph{global sections} of
1489	  \(\mathcal{F}\) over the intersections, an object of abelian groups (the underlying
1490	  additive structure of the sections). The two must not be conflated: the
1491	  presheaf-level acyclicity argument below runs entirely on this section complex.
1492	\end{definition}

1494	\begin{lemma}
1495	[The free complex represents the {\v C}ech complex]

1509	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-cech-map-into}
1510	  % (statement + proof) (read from references/stacks-cohomology.tex,
1511	  % L1163--1196).
1512	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Then there is an isomorphism
1513	  % $$
1514	  % \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet, \mathcal{F})
1515	  % =
1516	  % \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})
1517	  % $$
1518	  % functorial in $\mathcal{F} \in \Ob(\textit{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X))$."
1519	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology,
1520	  \texttt{lemma-cech-map-into}.}
1521	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an
1522	  open covering. For every presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{F}\)
1523	  there is an isomorphism of cochain complexes of \emph{abelian groups}
1524	  \[
1525	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}\bigl(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet, \mathcal{F}\bigr)
1526	      \;=\;
1527	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}),
1528	  \]
1529	  natural in \(\mathcal{F} \in \mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\). Here the left side is
1530	  the cochain complex obtained by applying the contravariant
1531	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(-, \mathcal{F})\) to the chain complex
1532	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex},
1533	  and the right side is the section {\v C}ech complex of
1534	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex}.
1535	\end{lemma}

1537	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "We saw in the discussion just above the lemma that
1538	% $$
1539	% \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(
1540	% (j_{i_0\ldots i_p})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0\ldots i_p}},
1541	% \mathcal{F})
1542	% =
1543	% \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0\ldots i_p}).
1544	% $$
1545	% Hence we see that it is indeed the case that the direct sum
1546	% $$
1547	% \bigoplus\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_p}
1548	% (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}
1549	% $$
1550	% represents the functor
1551	% $$
1552	% \mathcal{F}
1553	% \longmapsto
1554	% \prod\nolimits_{i_0\ldots i_p} \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0\ldots i_p}).
1555	% $$
1556	% Hence by Categories, Yoneda Lemma \ref{categories-lemma-yoneda}
1557	% we see that there is a complex $K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet$ with terms
1558	% as given. It is a simple matter to see that the maps are as given
1559	% in the lemma."
1560	\begin{proof}
1561	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:section_cech_complex}
1562	  The free–forgetful adjunction for presheaves of modules gives, composed with the
1563	  Yoneda isomorphism \(\operatorname{Hom}(\mathbf{y}\,V, \mathcal{F}) \cong
1564	  \mathcal{F}(V)\), the natural identification, for each multi-index,
1565	  \[
1566	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}\bigl(
1567	      \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}),\, \mathcal{F}
1568	    \bigr)
1569	    \;\cong\;
1570	    \operatorname{Hom}\bigl(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p},\,
1571	      \mathcal{F}\bigr)
1572	    \;\cong\;
1573	    \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}),
1574	  \]
1575	  where the last group is the evaluation of \(\mathcal{F}\) at the open
1576	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\). Taking the product over multi-indices,
1577	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(K(\mathcal{U})_p, \mathcal{F}) =
1578	  \prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}) =
1579	  \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\); the direct sum
1580	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_p\) therefore represents the functor \(\mathcal{F} \mapsto
1581	  \prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\). Under this
1582	  identification the \((-1)^j\) maps \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0
1583	  \ldots i_{p+1}}) \to \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j}
1584	  \ldots i_{p+1}})\) defining the differential of \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) become
1585	  exactly the alternating-sum restriction differential of
1586	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\), so the two cochain
1587	  complexes coincide; naturality in \(\mathcal{F}\) is immediate from the
1588	  naturality of the free–forgetful adjunction and of Yoneda. The per-multi-index
1589	  bijection \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,
1590	  U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}), \mathcal{F}) \cong \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\) is additive,
1591	  so it is promoted to an isomorphism of abelian groups (an \emph{additive}
1592	  equivalence); taking the product over multi-indices and assembling these degreewise
1593	  additive isomorphisms — which commute with the alternating-sum differentials — yields
1594	  the complex isomorphism by matching components degree by degree.
1595	\end{proof}

1613	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1614	  % (read from references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1200--1204).
1615	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a ringed space.
1616	  % Let $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i$ be an open covering.
1617	  % Let $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U} \subset \mathcal{O}_X$
1618	  % be the image presheaf of the map

1620	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}.}
1621	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an open
1622	  covering. The \emph{cover structure presheaf} \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}} :
1623	  X.\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}\) is the image presheaf of the augmentation
1624	  \[
1625	    \coprod_i \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_i) \;\longrightarrow\; \mathbf{1},
1626	  \]
1627	  where the target \(\mathbf{1} = \operatorname{PresheafOfModules.unit}(
1628	  X.\mathrm{ringCatSheaf}.\mathrm{obj})\) is the structure presheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X\)
1629	  regarded as a presheaf of modules over itself. Concretely
1630	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)\) when \(W\) is contained in some
1631	  \(U_i\), and \(0\) otherwise. The degree-\(0\) term
1632	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_0 = \coprod_i \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_i)\) of the free
1633	  presheaf complex of Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex} carries the
1634	  augmentation map \(K(\mathcal{U})_0 \to \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) onto this image
1635	  presheaf.
1636	\end{definition}

1638	\begin{lemma}
1639	[Objectwise detection of quasi-isomorphisms of presheaf-of-module complexes]
1640	  \label{lem:quasiIso_of_evaluation}
1641	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.quasiIso_of_evaluation,
1642	    AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_Fmap_homologyMap,
1643	    AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_of_evaluation}
1644	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
1645	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1646	  % (proof, the sectionwise-reduction step) (read from
1647	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1220--1226).
1648	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "It suffices to show that taking sections of this extended
1649	  % complex over any open $W \subset X$ leads to an acyclic complex."
1650	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1651	  (sectionwise reduction).}
1652	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of rings on a category \(C\) and let
1653	  \(\varphi : K \to L\) be a morphism of chain complexes in the abelian category
1654	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) of presheaves of \(R\)-modules. Suppose that for \emph{every}
1655	  object \(V\) the evaluated morphism — the image of \(\varphi\) under the functor
1656	  \(\bigl(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\bigr).
1657	  \operatorname{mapHomologicalComplex}\), a morphism of complexes of
1658	  \(R(V)\)-modules — is a quasi-isomorphism. Then \(\varphi\) is a
1659	  quasi-isomorphism. This is the objectwise-reduction step that turns the
1660	  resolution claim of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso} into a family of
1661	  sectionwise (combinatorial) statements: homology in \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) is
1662	  computed open-by-open, because the evaluation functors
1663	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\) are exact (hence preserve
1664	  homology) and jointly conservative. The Lean entry point is
1665	  \(\operatorname{quasiIso\_of\_evaluation}\), which combines the joint
1666	  conservativity of the evaluations (each \(g\) is an isomorphism as soon as every
1667	  \((\operatorname{evaluation} R\, V).\operatorname{map} g\) is) with the fact that
1668	  each \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\) preserves homology.
1669	\end{lemma}
1670	
1671	\begin{proof}
1672	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
1673	  A morphism of complexes is a quasi-isomorphism iff it induces an isomorphism on
1674	  homology in every degree (\(\operatorname{quasiIso\_iff}\),
1675	  \(\operatorname{quasiIsoAt\_iff\_isIso\_homologyMap}\)). Fix a degree \(i\). The
1676	  homology map \(H_i(\varphi)\) is an isomorphism in \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) iff each of
1677	  its evaluations is an isomorphism, since the underlying
1678	  presheaf-of-abelian-groups functor reflects isomorphisms and a morphism of
1679	  presheaves is an isomorphism iff it is so objectwise (joint conservativity). For
1680	  each \(V\), evaluation preserves homology, so the evaluation of \(H_i(\varphi)\)
1681	  is the homology map of the evaluated complex morphism; the latter is an
1682	  isomorphism because the evaluated morphism is a quasi-isomorphism by hypothesis.
1683	  Hence \(H_i(\varphi)\) is an isomorphism for all \(i\), i.e.\ \(\varphi\) is a
1684	  quasi-isomorphism.
1685	\end{proof}

1687	\begin{lemma}
1688	
1689	[Sectionwise description of the evaluated free complex]
1690	  \label{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}
1691	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEval_X,
1692	    AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le,
1693	    AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le}
1694	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:cover_structure_presheaf}
1695	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1696	  % (proof, the sectionwise description of $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$) (read from
1697	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1227--1252).
1698	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Write $I = I_1 \amalg I_2$ where $W \subset U_i$ if and only
1699	  % if $i \in I_1$. ... If $I_1 \not = \emptyset$, then
1700	  % $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)$ and
1701	  % $K^{ext}_p(W) = \bigoplus\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_p \in I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(W)$.
1702	  % This is true because of the simple description of the presheaves
1703	  % $(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}$."
1704	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1705	  (description of the evaluated complex).}
1706	  Fix an open \(V \subset X\) and write \(I_1 = I_1(V) = \{\,i \in I : V \leq U_i\,\}\)
1707	  for the set of cover members containing \(V\). Evaluating the free presheaf complex
1708	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex} at
1709	  \(V\) gives, degreewise,
1710	  \[
1711	    K(\mathcal{U})_p(V)
1712	      = \bigoplus_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I}
1713	        \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_\sigma)(V)
1714	      = \bigoplus_{\substack{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I \\ V \leq U_\sigma}}
1715	        \mathcal{O}_X(V)
1716	      = \bigoplus_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(V),
1717	  \]
1718	  because \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,W)(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) when
1719	  \(V \leq W\) and \(0\) otherwise (poset hom-set is a subsingleton), and
1720	  \(V \leq U_\sigma = U_{\sigma(0)} \cap \cdots \cap U_{\sigma(p)}\) holds iff
1721	  \(\sigma\) takes all its values in \(I_1\). Under this identification the
1722	  differential of \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet(V)\) is the alternating-sum combinatorial
1723	  {\v C}ech differential of the full simplex on the index set \(I_1\) with
1724	  coefficients in the single module \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\). Moreover the evaluated
1725	  augmentation target is \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) when
1726	  \(I_1 \neq \varnothing\) and \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = 0\) when
1727	  \(I_1 = \varnothing\). Thus the evaluation at \(V\) of the augmented complex
1728	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet \to \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}[0]\)
1729	  (\(\operatorname{cechFreeComplexAug}\)) is precisely the augmented combinatorial
1730	  {\v C}ech complex of the simplex on \(I_1\) with coefficients \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\).
1731	  The Lean entry point for the degree-\(p\) unfolding is
1732	  \(\operatorname{cechFreePresheafComplex\_X}\) (which exposes
1733	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_p = \coprod_\sigma \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_\sigma)\) by
1734	  \(\operatorname{rfl}\)) composed with
1735	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation}\) at \(V\). The full degreewise
1736	  identification is assembled from three pieces: \(\operatorname{cechFreeEval\_X}\)
1737	  (evaluation at \(V\) commutes with the coproduct, giving
1738	  \((\operatorname{eval} V)(K(\mathcal{U})_p) \cong \coprod_\sigma
1739	  (\operatorname{eval} V)(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_\sigma))\)),
1740	  \(\operatorname{freeYonedaEval\_iso\_of\_le}\) (a surviving summand with
1741	  \(V \leq U_\sigma\) is \(\cong \mathcal{O}_X(V)\)), and
1742	  \(\operatorname{freeYonedaEval\_isZero\_of\_not\_le}\) (a summand with
1743	  \(V \not\leq U_\sigma\) is the zero object); collapsing the coproduct over the
1744	  surviving \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1\) gives
1745	  \(\bigoplus_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(V)\).
1746	\end{lemma}
1747	
1748	\begin{proof}
1749	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:cover_structure_presheaf}
1750	  The degreewise identity is the value at \(V\) of the explicit description of the
1751	  free presheaf \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,W)\) recorded in
1752	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}: it sends \(V\) to the free
1753	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-module on \(\operatorname{Hom}(V, W)\), which is
1754	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) if \(V \leq W\) and \(0\) otherwise. Applying
1755	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation}\;V\) to the coproduct decomposition
1756	  \(\operatorname{cechFreePresheafComplex\_X}\) commutes with the direct sum and
1757	  collapses each summand to \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) or \(0\) according to whether
1758	  \(V \leq U_\sigma\), i.e.\ whether \(\sigma\) factors through \(I_1\); the surviving
1759	  summands are exactly those indexed by \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1\). The
1760	  evaluated faces are the index-dropping restriction maps, which on the surviving
1761	  summands are identities of \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\), so the alternating sum is the
1762	  combinatorial {\v C}ech differential. The augmentation value follows from
1763	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) exactly when some \(U_i\)
1764	  contains \(V\) (\(I_1 \neq \varnothing\)), recorded in
1765	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_structure_presheaf}.
1766	\end{proof}

1789	  % is given by

1792	  % \sum\nolimits_{j = 0, \ldots, p + 1} \sum\nolimits_{i \in I_1}
1793	  % (-1)^j s_{i_0 \ldots i_{j - 1} i i_j \ldots i_p}.
1794	  % $$
1795	  % The sum is finite as the element $s$ has finite support.
1796	  % Fix an element $i_{\text{fix}} \in I_1$. Define a map
1797	  % $$
1798	  % h : K^{ext}_p(W) \longrightarrow K^{ext}_{p + 1}(W)
1799	  % $$
1800	  % by the rule
1801	  % $h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p + 1}} =
1802	  % \left\{ \begin{matrix}
1803	  % 0 & \text{if} & i_0 \not = i_{\text{fix}} \\
1804	  % s_{i_1 \ldots i_{p + 1}} & \text{if} & i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}
1805	  % \end{matrix} \right.$"
1806	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1807	  (differential and contracting homotopy of the evaluated complex).}
1808	  Fix a nonempty index set \(J\) (in the application \(J = I_1(V)\)), a chosen index
1809	  \(i_{\mathrm{fix}} \in J\), and a single coefficient module \(M\). The
1810	  \emph{constant-coefficient combinatorial {\v C}ech engine} consists of: the
1811	  alternating-sum {\v C}ech differential \(\operatorname{combDifferential}\) on the
1812	  full simplex over \(J\) with coefficients \(M\),
1813	  \[
1814	    (\operatorname{combDifferential} t)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}
1815	      = \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\, t_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}},
1816	  \]
1817	  which squares to zero, \(\operatorname{combDifferential} \circ
1818	  \operatorname{combDifferential} = 0\) (\(\operatorname{combDifferential\_comp}\)); the
1819	  \emph{prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)} homotopy \(\operatorname{combHomotopy}\),
1820	  \((\operatorname{combHomotopy}\, i_{\mathrm{fix}}\, u)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}
1821	  = u_{i_1 \ldots i_{p+1}}\) when \(i_0 = i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) and \(0\) otherwise; and
1822	  the contracting identity \(d \circ h + h \circ d = \mathrm{id}\)
1823	  (\(\operatorname{combHomotopy\_spec}\)), which yields positive-degree exactness
1824	  \(\operatorname{combDifferential\_exact}\) and the cocycle-splitting form
1825	  \(\operatorname{combDifferential\_eq\_of\_cocycle}\). The remaining declarations are
1826	  sign- and index-bookkeeping consumed by the contracting identity: the
1827	  succ-shift reindexing \(\operatorname{cons\_comp\_succAbove\_succ}\) and the
1828	  alternating-sign flip \(\operatorname{combSign\_flip}\), together with the
1829	  degenerate-case simplification \(\operatorname{combHomotopy\_zero}\). The generic
1830	  category-theory helper \(\operatorname{isZero\_sigma\_of\_forall\_isZero}\) (a
1831	  coproduct of zero objects is a zero object) is recorded here as well, used to collapse
1832	  the empty-index direct sums of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_empty}. This engine is
