2827	\begin{lemma}
2828	[Cohomology sheaf is the sheafification of the objectwise homology]
2829	  \label{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
2830	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify,
2831	    PresheafOfModules.counitComplexIso,
2832	    PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdditive,
2833	    CategoryTheory.Functor.mapHomologyIso'}
2834	  % NOTE: project-built generalization. Stacks Tag~01XJ
2835	  % (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}) establishes the consequence
2836	  % $R^if_*\mathcal{F} \cong \text{sheafification of }(V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V),\mathcal{F}))$
2837	  % but does not state sheafification commuting with the homology of an arbitrary
2838	  % cochain complex as a standalone lemma. No verbatim Stacks source quote for this block.
2839	  Let \(K\) be a cochain complex of presheaves of modules over a site admitting
2840	  sheafification. For every index \(i\) there is a natural
2841	  isomorphism between the \(i\)-th homology of the sheafified complex and the
2842	  sheafification of the \(i\)-th objectwise (presheaf-level) homology of \(K\),
2843	  \[
2844	    H^i\bigl(\operatorname{sheafify}(K)\bigr)
2845	      \;\cong\;
2846	    \operatorname{sheafify}\bigl(H^i(K)\bigr).
2847	  \]
2848	  Equivalently, sheafification commutes with the formation of homology. This is the
2849	  reusable engine underlying the presheaf description of the higher direct images
2850	  (Stacks Tag~01XJ): it is what turns the resolution-internal cohomology sheaf into a
2851	  sheafification of an explicit cohomology presheaf.
2852	\end{lemma}
2853	
2854	\begin{proof}
2855	  Sheafification is exact: as the left adjoint of the inclusion of sheaves into
2856	  presheaves it preserves all colimits, and on a site admitting sheafification it
2857	  additionally preserves finite limits, so it preserves both the kernels and the
2858	  cokernels through which homology is computed. An exact additive functor commutes with
2859	  the homology functor of a cochain complex; chasing this through the counit complex
2860	  isomorphism \(\operatorname{sheafify}(\iota K) \cong K\) (the inclusion–sheafification
2861	  adjunction counit, an isomorphism on sheaves) gives
2862	  \(H^i(K) \cong H^i(\operatorname{sheafify} K) \cong
2863	  \operatorname{sheafify}(H^i(K))\). The supporting bricks are the
2864	  additivity of sheafification, the counit complex isomorphism, and the
2865	  complex-level transport of the short-complex map–homology isomorphism along an
2866	  additive homology-preserving functor.
2867	\end{proof}

2879	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to Y$ be a morphism of ringed spaces.

2881	  % The sheaves $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ are the sheaves
2882	  % associated to the presheaves
2883	  % $$

2885	  % $$

2888	  % bounded below complex $\mathcal{F}^\bullet$."

2890	  (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}).}
2891	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{G}\) an
2892	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and assume the category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has
2893	  enough injective objects, so that the derived pushforward \(R^k f_*\) is defined.
2894	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
2895	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and push it forward degreewise to obtain the cochain complex
2896	  of presheaves of modules \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) on \(S\). For every \(k \geq 0\)
2897	  the higher direct image \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is isomorphic to the
2898	  \emph{sheafification of the presheaf of objectwise homology} of this pushed complex,
2899	  \[
2900	    R^k f_*\mathcal{G}
2901	      \;\cong\;
2902	    \operatorname{sheafify}\Bigl(\,V \longmapsto
2903	      H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)\,\Bigr),
2904	    \qquad V \subseteq S \text{ open},

2906	  the sheafification of the \(k\)-th presheaf-level homology of
2907	  \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\). As a closing remark, this gives the affine-local
2908	  vanishing criterion consumed downstream
2909	  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp} and
2910	  \ref{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic}): since the sheafification of a presheaf
2911	  vanishes exactly when that presheaf vanishes on a basis of opens,
2912	  \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G} = 0\) iff \(H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr) = 0\)
2913	  for every \(V\) in a basis of the topology of \(S\); the affine opens form such a
2914	  basis, so it suffices to check the vanishing on affine \(V\).
2915	
2916	  The identification of the displayed presheaf homology with the absolute cohomology
2917	  of the preimage,
2918	  \[
2919	    H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)
2920	      \;=\;
2921	    H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),

2924	  \(f^{-1}(V)\), is supplied at point of use; this lemma records the

2928	\begin{proof}
2929	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image, def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
2930	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
2931	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. By definition \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is the \(k\)-th
2932	  cohomology sheaf of the complex \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) of presheaves of modules
2933	  over \(S\). The comparison engine of
2934	  Lemma~\ref{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology} now applies: taking homology in
2935	  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules is the sheafification of the objectwise (presheaf-level)
2936	  homology, because the sheafification/inclusion adjunction is exact (it preserves the
2937	  finite limits and colimits computing kernels and cokernels). Hence
2938	  \[
2939	    R^k f_*\mathcal{G}
2940	      \;\cong\;
2941	    \operatorname{sheafify}\Bigl(\,V \longmapsto
2942	      H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)\,\Bigr),
2943	  \]
2944	  which is the stated isomorphism. Since the sheafification of a presheaf vanishes
2945	  exactly when the underlying presheaf vanishes on a basis of opens, this yields the
2946	  affine-local vanishing criterion.
2947	\end{proof}
2948	
2949	\begin{lemma}
2950	[Affine open immersions are $(\cdot)_*$-acyclic and degenerate the composition]
2951	  \label{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
2952	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp}
2953	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
2954	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
2955	  % \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative vanishing of higher direct
2956	  % images for affine morphisms) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
2957	  % L180--199).
2958	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
2959	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
2960	  % If $f$ is affine then $R^if_*\mathcal{F} = 0$ for all $i > 0$."
2961	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
2962	  \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing}.}
2963	  Let \(X\) be separated, \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) the open immersion of an affine
2964	  open \(U\), and \(f : X \to S\) any morphism, with composite \(g := f \circ j : U
2965	  \to S\). Then:
2966	  \begin{enumerate}
2967	    \item \(j\) is an affine morphism (an open immersion of an affine open into a
2968	      separated scheme), so its higher direct images vanish: \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} = 0\)
2969	      for all \(q \geq 1\) and every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module
2970	      \(\mathcal{H}\); and
2971	    \item consequently, for every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{H}\) on \(U\) and every
2972	      \(k \geq 0\),
2973	      \[
2974	        R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr) \;\cong\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H}.
2975	      \]
2976	  \end{enumerate}
2977	\end{lemma}
2978	
2979	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "According to
2980	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
2981	% the sheaf
2982	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
2983	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
2984	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is
2985	% affine, see Morphisms, Definition \ref{morphisms-definition-affine}.
2986	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} we conclude that
2987	% $H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0$
2988	% whenever $V$ is affine. Since $S$ has a basis consisting of affine
2989	% opens we win." (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L187--198)
2990	\begin{proof}
2991	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived}
2992	  (1) An open immersion of an affine open \(U\) into a separated scheme \(X\) is an
2993	  affine morphism: separatedness makes the immersion \(j\) quasi-separated and the
2994	  preimage of any affine open of \(X\) meets \(U\) in an affine, so \(j\) is affine.
2995	  The relative affine vanishing for affine morphisms then gives \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} =
2996	  0\) for \(q \geq 1\); concretely, by the presheaf description
2997	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}) \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H}\) is the
2998	  sheafification of \(V \mapsto H^q(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{H})\), and for affine \(V\) the
2999	  preimage \(j^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which the Serre vanishing of
3000	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} kills \(H^q\) for \(q \geq 1\).
3001	
3002	  (2) Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{H} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
3003	  \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-modules and apply \(j_*\) degreewise. Since each \(\mathcal{I}^n\)
3004	  is \(j_*\)-acyclic by part (1) (\(R^q j_*\mathcal{I}^n = 0\) for \(q \geq 1\)), the
3005	  complex \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is a resolution of \(j_*\mathcal{H}\).
3006	
3007	  Each \(j_*\mathcal{I}^n\) is \(f_*\)-acyclic. By the presheaf description
3008	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}), \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n)\) is the
3009	  sheafification of
3010	  \[
3011	    V \;\longmapsto\; H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), (j_*\mathcal{I}^n)|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr)
3012	      \;=\; H^k\bigl(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
3013	  \]
3014	  the equality because \((j_*\mathcal{I}^n)|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is the pushforward of
3015	  \(\mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\) along the open immersion
3016	  \(U \cap f^{-1}(V) \hookrightarrow f^{-1}(V)\), which leaves cohomology unchanged. For
3017	  \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A) \subseteq S\) affine, \(f^{-1}(V)\) is quasi-compact and,
3018	  \(X\) being separated and \(j\) affine, the affine open \(U\) meets it in the affine
3019	  open \(U \cap f^{-1}(V)\); hence by Serre vanishing
3020	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} the cohomology
3021	  \(H^k(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\).
3022	  Since the affine opens form a basis of \(S\), this forces
3023	  \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n) = 0\) for all \(k \geq 1\).
3024	
3025	  Thus \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is an \(f_*\)-acyclic resolution of
3026	  \(j_*\mathcal{H}\), and the acyclic-resolution comparison
3027	  Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} applied with \(G = f_*\) gives,
3028	  for every \(k\),
3029	  \[
3030	    R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr)
3031	      \;\cong\; H^k\bigl(f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)\bigr)
3032	      \;=\; H^k\bigl((f \circ j)_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\bigr)
3033	      \;=\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H},
3034	  \]
3035	  the middle equality because \(f_* \circ j_* = (f \circ j)_* = g_*\), and the last
3036	  because \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is an injective resolution of \(\mathcal{H}\)
3037	  computing \(R^k g_*\mathcal{H}\).
3038	\end{proof}
3039	
3040	\begin{lemma}
3041	[Each {\v C}ech term is $f_*$-acyclic]
3042	  \label{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic}
3043	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechTerm_pushforward_acyclic}
3044	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, def:cech_nerve, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
3045	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
3046	  % \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative vanishing of higher direct
3047	  % images for affine morphisms) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
3048	  % L180--199).
3049	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
3050	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
3051	  % If $f$ is affine then $R^if_*\mathcal{F} = 0$ for all $i > 0$."
3052	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
3053	  \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative affine vanishing).}
3054	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be separated and quasi-compact, \(\mathcal{F}\) a
3055	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and \(\mathfrak{U}\) a finite affine open
3056	  cover of \(X\) with all intersections affine. Then for every \(p \geq 0\) the
3057	  degree-\(p\) {\v C}ech term \(\mathcal{C}^p =
3058	  \prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\)
3059	  is right \((\,\cdot\,)_*\)-acyclic for the pushforward \(f_*\):
3060	  \[
3061	    (f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,k\,(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0
3062	      \qquad \text{for all } k \geq 1.
3063	  \]
3064	\end{lemma}
3065	
3066	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "According to
3067	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
3068	% the sheaf
3069	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
3070	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
3071	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is
3072	% affine, see Morphisms, Definition \ref{morphisms-definition-affine}.
3073	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} we conclude that
3074	% $H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0$
3075	% whenever $V$ is affine. Since $S$ has a basis consisting of affine
3076	% opens we win."
3077	\begin{proof}
3078	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, def:cech_nerve, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
3079	  A product of acyclic objects is acyclic, so it suffices to treat a single factor
3080	  \((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\), where \(s = (i_0, \ldots, i_p)\), \(U_s =
3081	  U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine, and \(j_s : U_s \hookrightarrow X\) is the open
3082	  immersion. Acyclicity for \(f_*\) is a local question on \(S\): the derived
3083	  pushforward \(R^k f_*\, \mathcal{G}\) is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
3084	  \(V \mapsto H^k(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)})\), so it vanishes for
3085	  \(k \geq 1\) iff that cohomology vanishes for all affine \(V \subseteq S\).
3086	  Restricting to such a \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), the base change \(f_V :
3087	  f^{-1}(V) \to V\) is again separated and quasi-compact over the separated base
3088	  \(V\), hence \(f^{-1}(V)\) is separated and the affine \(U_s\) meets it in the
3089	  affine open \(U_s \cap f^{-1}(V)\).
3090	
3091	  Now decompose the structure map \(g_s := f \circ j_s : U_s \to S\). First,
3092	  \(j_s\) is the inclusion of an affine open into the separated scheme \(X\), hence
3093	  an affine morphism, so the higher direct images \(R^q (j_s)_*\) vanish for
3094	  \(q \geq 1\). By Lemma~\ref{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp} part (2),
3095	  \(R^k f_*\bigl((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\bigr) \cong
3096	  R^k (g_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\). Second, working locally over an affine
3097	  \(V \subseteq S\) as above, \(g_s\) restricts to a morphism of affine schemes
3098	  \(U_s \cap f^{-1}(V) \to V\), which is an affine morphism; by the relative affine
3099	  vanishing \(R^k (g_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s}) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\). That relative
3100	  vanishing is itself a consequence of Serre vanishing
3101	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}): \(R^k(g_s)_*\mathcal{G}\) is the
3102	  sheafification of \(V \mapsto H^k(g_s^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G})\), and for affine
3103	  \(V\) the preimage \(g_s^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which higher cohomology of a
3104	  quasi-coherent sheaf vanishes. Combining, \(R^k f_*(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0\) for all
3105	  \(k \geq 1\).
3106	\end{proof}
3107	
3108	\begin{lemma}
3109	
3110	\leanok
3111	[{\v C}ech complex computes the higher direct images]
3112	  \label{lem:cech_computes_cohomology}
3113	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage}
3114	  \uses{lem:cech_augmented_resolution, lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic, lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived, def:cech_complex, def:higher_direct_image}
3115	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KE; Lemma
3116	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent} ({\v C}ech computes cohomology when
3117	  % all intersections are affine) and Lemma
3118	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}
3119	  % ($H^q(X,\mathcal{F}) = H^0(S, R^q f_*\mathcal{F})$ for affine $S$)
3120	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L245--256 and L843--854).
3121	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
3122	  % Let $\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i$ be an open covering such that
3123	  % $U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}$ is affine open for all $p \ge 0$ and all
3124	  % $i_0, \ldots, i_p \in I$.
3125	  % In this case for any quasi-coherent sheaf $\mathcal{F}$ we have
3126	  % $$
3127	  % \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^p(X, \mathcal{F})
3128	  % $$
3129	  % as $\Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$-modules for all $p$."
3130	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
3131	  % Assume that $f$ is quasi-separated and quasi-compact.
3132	  % Assume $S$ is affine.
3133	  % For any quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module $\mathcal{F}$
3134	  % we have
3135	  % $$
3136	  % H^q(X, \mathcal{F}) = H^0(S, R^qf_*\mathcal{F})
3137	  % $$
3138	  % for all $q \in \mathbf{Z}$."
3139	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KE
3140	  (\texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent}) and
3141	  \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}.}
3142	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a separated quasi-compact morphism of schemes, let
3143	  \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and let
3144	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover of
3145	  \(X\) (so, by separatedness, every intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is
3146	  affine). Then the cohomology sheaves of the relative {\v C}ech complex
3147	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
3148	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} compute the higher direct images: for every
3149	  \(i \geq 0\) there is a canonical isomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules
3150	  \[
3151	    H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr)
3152	      \;\cong\; R^i f_*\mathcal{F}.
3153	  \]
3154	  In particular, over an affine base \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), taking global
3155	  sections gives \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) = \check{H}^i(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}) =
3156	  H^0(S, R^i f_*\mathcal{F})\) as \(A\)-modules for all \(i\).
3157	
3158	  Two points about the precise form of the comparison should be noted. First, the
3159	  derived-functor higher direct image \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) on the right-hand side
3160	  is the one obtained from injective resolutions, which is available only when the
3161	  category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has enough injectives; accordingly this
3162	  comparison is asserted under the hypothesis that injective resolutions exist in
3163	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\), and it compares the (always-defined) {\v C}ech higher
3164	  direct image \(H^i(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}))\)
3165	  against the derived-functor one only in that case. Second, we assert the comparison in the
3166	  weak form of the \emph{existence} of an isomorphism, i.e.\ as
3167	  \(\bigl(H^i(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})) \cong
3168	  R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr)\) without singling out a chosen natural isomorphism.
3169	  This existence statement is all the downstream consumers (representability of the
3170	  relative Picard functor, local triviality of the comparison) require: they use
3171	  only that the unconditional {\v C}ech \(R^i f_*\) and the derived-functor
3172	  \(R^i f_*\) agree as objects, never a specific identification, so committing to a
3173	  canonical natural transformation would add coherence obligations that play no
3174	  role in the applications.
3175	\end{lemma}
3176	
3177	\begin{proof}
3178	  \uses{lem:cech_augmented_resolution, lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic, lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived, def:cech_complex, def:higher_direct_image}
3179	  Apply the abstract Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} with the
3180	  right-derivable additive functor \(G = f_*\)
3181	  (the pushforward \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X) \to \operatorname{QCoh}(S)\)), the object
3182	  \(A = \mathcal{F}\), and the resolution \(J^\bullet = \mathcal{C}^\bullet\) given by
3183	  the {\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}. The two hypotheses of the
3184	  abstract lemma are exactly the geometric sub-lemmas established above:
3185	  \begin{enumerate}
3186	    \item By Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_augmented_resolution} the augmented {\v C}ech
3187	      complex \(0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0 \to \mathcal{C}^1 \to \cdots\) is
3188	      exact, i.e.\ \(\mathcal{C}^\bullet\) is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in
3189	      \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
3190	    \item By Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic} each term
3191	      \(\mathcal{C}^p\) is right \(f_*\)-acyclic:
3192	      \((f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,k\,(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\).
3193	  \end{enumerate}
3194	  Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} then gives, for every \(i\), an
3195	  isomorphism between the \(i\)-th derived functor evaluated at \(\mathcal{F}\) and
3196	  the \(i\)-th cohomology of the complex obtained by applying \(f_*\) to the
3197	  resolution,
3198	  \[
3199	    (f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,i\,(\mathcal{F})
3200	      \;\cong\;
3201	    H^i\bigl(f_*\,\mathcal{C}^\bullet\bigr).
3202	  \]
3203	  By construction (Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex}) the complex \(f_*\,
3204	  \mathcal{C}^\bullet\) is precisely the relative {\v C}ech complex
3205	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\), so its \(i\)-th
3206	  cohomology is \(H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})
3207	  \bigr)\); and \((f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,i\,(\mathcal{F})\) is the
3208	  derived-functor higher direct image \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) of
3209	  Definition~\ref{def:higher_direct_image}. This yields the isomorphism
3210	  \(H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr) \cong
3211	  R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) asserted by the lemma; taking it in the existence
3212	  (\(\operatorname{Nonempty}\)) form discards the choice of resolution, as required.
3213	  Over an affine base \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), passing to global sections
3214	  recovers \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) = \check{H}^i(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^0(S,
3215	  R^i f_*\mathcal{F})\).
3216	\end{proof}
